headline: Agent memory and continuity emerged as the dominant theme this week, anchored by debates over context, durable logs, and production evaluation.
body: The week's clearest rotation centered on how coding agents retain context across runs, with Victor Savkin framing current agents as talented engineers with amnesia [0] and Ishaan Sehgal arguing that the durable session log, not the model or framework, is the persistent entity that defines an agent [2]. Tooling moved in parallel: Google Cloud detailed AI-native databases, hybrid search in Spanner, and data agents built on the Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol, surfacing vector database and Gemini among the top movers [6][10][11]. On infrastructure and architecture, Nishant Gupta of Meta Superintelligence Labs pushed continuous evaluation pipelines over static benchmarks [7], while Angie Jones argued engineering value is shifting to system architecture and decomposition [8]. Separately, reports tied Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models to government-forced removals and export scrutiny [1][9].

cited_items.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
